forecast-period.text: A trough of low pressure lies over the far northeast. A ridge of high pressure builds in the west. A cold front will move over the south late Monday and early Tuesday, followed by a high pressure system, which will linger in the Bight midweek. The high will move into Bass Strait towards the end of the week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Areas of morning fog. Isolated showers about the far north. Isolated showers about the agricultural area becoming scattered during the afternoon and evening. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds, tending west to northwesterly about the south during the day.
Isolated showers about the agricultural areas and the far north. Possible thunderstorms about the far north. Southwest to southeasterly winds.

Morning fog patches about the agricultural area. Isolated showers about the agricultural area, most likely about southern coasts and ranges. Maximum temperatures near to below average. South to southwesterly winds fresh about southern coasts.

Morning fog patches about the agricultural area. Isolated showers about the agricultural area, contracting to southern coasts in the evening. Maximum temperatures near to below average. South to southwesterly winds fresh about southern coasts. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds tending easterly in the west.

text.p: Morning fog patches about the agricultural area. Mostly dry, apart form light showers about southern coasts. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds tending easterly in the west.
Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Thursday: 1 to 5 mm about the south of the pastoral districts and agricultural area, 2 to 5 mm about southern coasts and ranges and the far north.
